article_title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close before May 27 | TechCrunch

article_items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close before May 27 | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. Get VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.
article_items.excerpt: The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27. Get VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100,000. Apply now.
article_items.summary: The network runs so deep that alumni have even acquired each other: Dropbox acquired fellow Battlefield 200 alum DocSend in 2021. The companies that define categories rarely start polished Some of the most consequential companies in tech history didn’t launch with splashy fundraising announcements. The practical impact: You don’t need to make the top 20 for this experience to change your trajectory. This is your opportunity to gain investor exposure, receive direct VC feedback, and prove your company belongs among the next generation of category-defining startups. Watch next: Startup Battlefield 200 has never been a competition for the most polished companies. The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27.
article_items.what_happened: The network runs so deep that alumni have even acquired each other: Dropbox acquired fellow Battlefield 200 alum DocSend in 2021. The companies that define categories rarely start polished Some of the most consequential companies in tech history didn’t launch with splashy fundraising announcements.

key_points: The network runs so deep that alumni have even acquired each other: Dropbox acquired fellow Battlefield 200 alum DocSend in 2021.
The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is May 27.
The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30.
Apply today for the opportunity to take the TechCrunch Disrupt Stage alongside 200 of the world’s most promising early-stage startups.
The companies that define categories rarely start polished Some of the most consequential companies in tech history didn’t launch with splashy fundraising announcements.
Together, they’ve raised over $32 billion and generated more than 250 exits, including acquisitions by Microsoft, Google, Salesforce, Uber, and Amazon.
They all passed through the same crucible: Startup Battlefield 200.
Startup Battlefield 200 has never been a competition for the most polished companies.
The Startup Battlefield 200 track record speaks for itself More than 1,700 companies have competed in Startup Battlefield 200.
One startup takes the crown and wins $100,000 in equity-free funding.
More from TechCrunch Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in days: Apply before May 27 The deadline to apply or nominate for Startup Battlefield 200 is Friday, May 27.
Just 20 finalists pitch live on the Disrupt Stage.
